cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the best answer for this question. The passage is about the London Olympics and whether it was a success, especially focusing on its environmental efforts. The main point the author is making is that London could win a gold medal for hosting the "greenest" Games ever. Looking at the content, the article talks about several green initiatives. They mention cleaning up polluted areas, using recycled materials for construction, green building techniques, waste management with rainwater and recycling, and using public transport to reduce carbon emissions. There's also a part about the anti-doping success, but that's a smaller section. The question is asking for the best title of the passage. The options are A through D. Let me look at each option: A) How to deal with the anti-doping - This is only a small part of the passage. The anti-doping is mentioned, but it's not the main focus. So probably not A. B) How to use recycled materials - While using recycled materials is discussed, the article covers more than just that. There are other green initiatives like building techniques, waste management, and public transport. So B might be too narrow. C) London's green games - This seems to encapsulate the main idea. The whole passage is about London's efforts to make the Olympics as green as possible. The first paragraph mentions the "greenest" Games, and the rest of the article supports that claim with various examples. This looks like a strong candidate.
